name: Cueva del Diable — circular desde Sant Martí de Tous
region_intro: Jogging around Sant Martí De Tous offers a variety of routes through a landscape characterized by rolling hills, dense woodlands, and open fields. The region features a mix of rural paths and trails that often lead to historical points of interest like castles. Elevation changes are common, providing both gentle and more challenging running experiences. Best jogging routes around Sant Martí De Tous The most popular jogging route is Castillo de Miralles y L'Aubereda — circular desde Sant Martí de Tous, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete, exploring historical sites and natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Cueva del Diable — circular desde Sant Martí de Tous, a moderate 4.0 miles (6.5 km) path that winds through varied terrain, including a notable cave area. Local runners also love the Fields near l'Aubareda – Devil's Cave loop from Sant Martí de Tous, a 5.8 miles (9.4 km) trail leading through open fields and wooded sections, often completed in about 1 hour 16 minutes. Jogging around Sant Martí De Tous is defined by its rural paths, historical castles, and diverse forest trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogging routes to more challenging trails with significant elevation gain. The routes in Sant Martí De Tous are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Sant Martí De Tous's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Sant Martí De Tous?
A: There are 15 dedicated jogging routes around Sant Martí De Tous, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes for beginners in Sant Martí De Tous?
A: Yes, Sant Martí De Tous offers at least one easy jogging route. The region's landscape includes gentle paths suitable for those new to running or looking for a less strenuous workout.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails around Sant Martí De Tous?
A: The jogging trails in Sant Martí De Tous feature a diverse landscape of rolling hills, dense woodlands, and open fields. You'll find a mix of rural paths and trails, often with elevation changes that provide both gentle and more challenging running experiences.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Sant Martí De Tous?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Sant Martí De Tous are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1113652853/cueva-del-diable-circular-desde-sant-marti-de-tous" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Diable — circular desde Sant Martí de Tous&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.5 km path, or the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1113642350/castillo-de-miralles-y-l-aubereda-circular-desde-sant-marti-de-tous" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Miralles y L'Aubereda — circular desde Sant Martí de Tous&lt;/a&gt;, which is 15.1 km.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails with significant elevation gain?
A: Absolutely. Sant Martí De Tous has 5 difficult jogging routes, designed for those seeking a more intense workout. These trails often feature significant elevation changes as they wind through the region's hilly terrain.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or attractions I can see while running?
A: Many routes pass by historical points of interest and offer scenic views. You might encounter landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2991030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miralles Castle&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy panoramic vistas from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1514317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Rubió&lt;/a&gt;. The region is known for its castles and natural beauty.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Sant Martí De Tous?
A: The running trails in Sant Martí De Tous are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, historical sites, and the mix of woodlands and open fields.

Q: Are there any longer distance running routes available?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer runs, Sant Martí De Tous offers several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1113642350/castillo-de-miralles-y-l-aubereda-circular-desde-sant-marti-de-tous" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Miralles y L'Aubereda — circular desde Sant Martí de Tous&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 15.1 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17554688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Martí de Tous Castle – Fields near l'Aubareda loop from Sant Martí de Tous&lt;/a&gt; covers 13.5 km.

Q: What is the best season for jogging in Sant Martí De Tous?
A: Sant Martí De Tous is enjoyable for jogging throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Even in winter, the trails can be beautiful, though some routes might be more exposed to the elements.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by interesting historical sites?
A: Many routes incorporate historical elements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14954449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Cave – Sant Martí de Tous Castle loop from Sant Martí de Tous&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the castle, offering a glimpse into the region's past while you run.

Q: How many people typically explore the jogging routes in this area?
A: Over 700 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain of Sant Martí De Tous, indicating a popular and well-loved area for jogging enthusiasts.
tips: Close to Aubareda is this set of cultivated fields interspersed with forests that let you see the mountains on the other side of the Ódena Basin in the background.

The Devil's Cave is a rock formation in the municipality of Sant Martí de Tous. From above you can see the hole made by the river and you can also go inside, entering the cave following the stream of water.
